content:
I want to start contributing for this project, and I was wondering how should I set up my development workflow.

  • When you're developing the kernel, do you run the kernel in a vm, or do you just run the tests?
  • Could you maybe share a link to this guide? I was searching for it but i couldn't find the right keywords..

I did some kernel development before, but I didn't think that I did it properly. For example, to test my codes, I only run a vm and tested the changes manually there, and I didn't use the kernel unit tests. I would really appreciate your inputs!

How I test stuff depends heavily on the details. Generally, the changes I make are for drivers that will run in the Android kernel, so if I'm uncertain about a patch, I'll cherry-pick it into my Android tree and see if it works when used together with the real driver that it is intended for. This may involve running it on an Android emulator or a real phone. For many patches, I'm happy if it passes make LLVM=1 CLIPPY=1 and make LLVM=1 rustdoc.
